key_themes: Solid revenue growth (+4% FY25, accelerating in H2) and market share gains
Strategic US Molson Coors partnership enabling scalable future growth despite short-term transition costs
Margin/EBITDA pressure and 16% profit decline from one-offs, investments, and packaging levy
Successful diversification (45% revenue non-tonic) and brand strength
Frequent undervaluation calls, insider buying, and analyst upside targets
summary: Fevertree delivered respectable revenue growth in FY25 with improving momentum, H2 acceleration, further diversification, and strong US brand performance during the Molson Coors transition, but reported a 16% drop in adjusted EBITDA/profit due to margin compression, transition costs, and a one-off packaging levy charge. The company expressed comfort with 2026 expectations and highlighted strategic progress. Overall investor and media sentiment is cautiously positive, centered on long-term brand strength, the US opportunity, and repeated 'undervalued' narratives from outlets like Simply Wall St, supported by recent share price strength (~830-850p range, up ~9% in recent weeks) and analyst targets implying moderate upside.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/FEVR.L/)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/FEVR.L/)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/FEVR.L/news/)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/fevertree-fy25-presentation-us-partnership-drives-growth-amid-margin-pressure-93CH-4577265)
sources: Web/news: 20+ results including Yahoo Finance, Reuters, company RNS, Simply Wall St, FT, Investing.com, X/Twitter: ~30 posts returned (low stock-specific volume; mostly consumer/product mentions with ~3-5 investor-relevant), Investor forums: ADVFN, LSE.co.uk, Stockopedia discussion boards referenced, Earnings reports and analyst notes from March 2026 FY25 results
